name: Bill's Navy Bean Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over the dried beans and discard any debris.
Wash and drain. Put beans in a large pot and cover with water. Let stand 8 hours or overnight.
2. The next day, chop celery, carrots, and onion into 1/4-inch pieces (easier with a food processor). Drain beans. Add vegetables, 5 cups of water, ham hocks or ham bone, and pepper to taste. Add salt if desired. Bring to a simmer and cook over low heat for 3 hours or until beans are tender.
3. Remove ham hocks and set aside to cool. Remove meat from bones and cut into half-inch pieces.
4. Put 2 cups of the soup into a blender along with margarine and flour; puree. Pour this puree back into the pan. Puree more of the soup if you prefer a smoother texture. Add the chopped ham pieces back into the soup. Simmer for 15-20 minutes until slightly thickened.
Yield: 6-8 large servings. This soup is especially good when sprinkled with a few drops of hot pepper sauce just before serving.
